headline: Survey at 0.6% completion: urgent acceleration needed across 8 regions to reach 1,350 target.
collection_status: Only 8 responses collected against 1,350 target (0.59% completion). Active in just 2 of 10 regions (MARA: 5, SONGWE: 3). Current pace would deliver ~480 responses by survey close—68% short of target. Volunteer-heavy sample (75%) lacks institutional balance.
key_findings: Stakeholders prioritize new program development (5 mentions) over KiuFunza expansion (3 mentions), signaling demand for innovation beyond current scope
Partner satisfaction high at 4.8/5, but scale readiness low at 3.0/5—partners value work but doubt capacity for regional expansion
All 8 qualitative responses focus on teacher incentives, government policy integration, and digital monitoring—core operational gaps blocking scale
Zero coverage in 8 of 10 regions; MARA and SONGWE concentration suggests limited outreach infrastructure outside two pilot zones
flags: Critical: 99.4% of survey target unmet; current trajectory misses deadline by 870 responses. Activate contingency sampling plan immediately.
Sample composition risk: 75% volunteers vs. 25% regional coordinators skews feedback toward grassroots; under-represents institutional stakeholder views needed for policy recommendations.
Geographic blind spot: 8 silent regions may harbor unmapped barriers (access, connectivity, engagement) that low response rates are masking.
recommendation: Immediately deploy targeted outreach to remaining 8 regions with pre-identified regional coordinator and institutional partner contacts. Rebalance respondent mix toward 40% RCs/institutional actors to strengthen policy-relevance of findings for government integration strategy.
